title: Where Every Top 100 Prospect is Expected to Start the 2024 MLB Season.
body:
As the 2026 Major League Baseball season is set to kick off, fans are eagerly anticipating the emergence of top prospects who could make a significant impact on the league. In a promising development, three highly-touted players from the MLB Pipeline's Top 100 list – Kevin McGonigle, JJ Wetherholt, and Carson Benge – are expected to begin the season as starting players for their respective teams. This marks a significant milestone for these talented young players, who have consistently impressed scouts and analysts with their skills and potential.

The inclusion of McGonigle, Wetherholt, and Benge as starting players is a testament to the growing emphasis on developing young talent within the league. These players, ranked second, fifth, and 16th respectively on the MLB Pipeline's Top 100 list, have demonstrated exceptional skills on the field, and their promotion to starting roles is a vote of confidence in their abilities. McGonigle, a versatile infielder, has shown a keen eye for hitting and impressive defensive skills, while Wetherholt has impressed with his power hitting and athleticism. Benge, a talented third baseman, has consistently demonstrated a strong bat and exceptional fielding prowess.

The inclusion of these top prospects at the starting level also marks a significant shift in the league's approach to player development. Gone are the days of slow and deliberate promotions, where young players were often relegated to minor league stints for extended periods. Today, teams are more focused on giving top prospects an opportunity to contribute early in their careers, with the hope that they can make a meaningful impact on the field. This approach not only benefits the players themselves, but also creates a more competitive and engaging product for fans.

The presence of these top prospects in starting roles also has significant implications for the teams they play for. With the increasing importance of youth and talent in modern baseball, teams are investing heavily in their farm systems and development programs. The successful integration of top prospects like McGonigle, Wetherholt, and Benge can be a major factor in determining a team's success, as they bring fresh energy, enthusiasm, and talent to the field. Moreover, their presence can also create a ripple effect, inspiring other young players to push themselves and strive for excellence.

As the 2026 season gets underway, fans can expect to see a renewed focus on developing young talent and giving top prospects an opportunity to shine. With the likes of McGonigle, Wetherholt, and Benge leading the charge, the future of baseball looks bright and exciting. As the league continues to evolve and adapt to changing trends and circumstances, one thing is clear – the next generation of stars is ready to take center stage.

As the season progresses, fans will be closely watching these top prospects to see how they adapt to the pressures and demands of the major leagues. Will they rise to the challenge, or will they face the inevitable growing pains that come with transitioning to the highest level of competition? Whatever the outcome, one thing is certain – the 2026 season promises to be an exciting and unpredictable ride, full of surprises and opportunities for growth and development.
